event: Boy and dog gazing at a river at twilight
symbols: still water at dusk — the peace that passes understanding, a moment of quiet communion with creation
boy gazing outward — youthful contemplation before the vastness of nature
fading light — the liminal threshold between day and night, the transience of life
description: A figure of a boy seen from behind sits among tall brown reeds at the edge of a wide, calm river. A light-colored dog stands beside him, also facing the water. The far shore shows a low line of dark mountains beneath a twilight sky streaked with blue, pink, and gray clouds. The water reflects the fading light. The overall palette is cool and muted, dominated by blues and earth tones.

_meta.title: Dog, Boy, and St. John River
